steps.title: Extract the linear model from the graph
steps.content: Two readable points from the graph: $(0,\, 65)$ and $(100,\, 105)$. The slope is $$\frac{105 - 65}{100 - 0} = \frac{40}{100} = \frac{2}{5}.$$ So the model is $t(x) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}x$.

steps.title: Check choice A (slope = 2/5)
steps.content:
For every increase of $x$ by 1, $t(x)$ increases by $\frac{2}{5}$. The slope is exactly $\frac{2}{5}$.
  • Choice A is TRUE — eliminate.

steps.title: Check choice B (GC = 0% gives 60–65°C)
steps.content:
At $x = 0$: $t(0) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(0) = 65^\circ C$. The value $65^\circ C$ falls in the interval $(60^\circ C,\, 65^\circ C]$.
  • Choice B is TRUE — eliminate.

steps.title: Check choice C (GC = 95% gives 100–105°C) and choice D (GC = 98% gives 80–90°C)
steps.content:
At $x = 95$: $t(95) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(95) = 65 + 38 = 103^\circ C$. That is between $100^\circ C$ and $105^\circ C$, so choice C is true.

At $x = 98$: $t(98) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(98) = 65 + 39.2 = 104.2^\circ C$. Choice D claims this temperature is between $80^\circ C$ and $90^\circ C$, but $104.2^\circ C$ is far outside that range.
  • Choice C is TRUE — eliminate.
  • Choice D is FALSE. That matches choice D — D is correct.
